jokes.text: Well, I lit a patchouli candle, so hopefully it'll... calm your chi.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn is clearly stressed and frazzled about her morning
jokes.punchline: David's absurdly inadequate solution of lighting a patchouli candle
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - David's pretentious, new-age solution to practical problems. Well-structured with his confident delivery contrasting with the inadequacy of the solution.

jokes.text: Somebody decided to do a synchronized swimming routine at 2am.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explaining why she didn't sleep well
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of her unborn baby doing synchronized swimming in utero
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy - the specificity of 'synchronized swimming' makes it much funnier than generic 'kicking around.' Great economy and originality.

jokes.text: Something to do with her husband's heart. - Oh, well, hope it clears itself up. - Yeah, well, it won't. That's the problem. - Well, you never know. - Well, I do.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn mentions her sister can't come due to husband's heart problem
jokes.punchline: David's oblivious optimism about a serious heart condition, followed by Jocelyn's blunt certainty
jokes.explanation: David's characteristic inability to handle serious situations properly, contrasted with Jocelyn's matter-of-fact acceptance of tragedy.

jokes.text: Sounds very dark.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn announces she's throwing her own baby shower
jokes.punchline: David's understated reaction to what is indeed a sad situation
jokes.explanation: Classic David deadpan reaction. Perfect economy - two words that capture the absurdity of the situation.

jokes.text: Roland's been trying to help me, offered off his movie posters, you know, but I just don't think that 'The Pelican Brief' is festive enough for a baby shower.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn talking about decorating challenges
jokes.punchline: The hilariously inappropriate suggestion of using 'The Pelican Brief' poster for baby shower decor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity - 'The Pelican Brief' is such a random, inappropriate choice for baby shower decor. Shows Roland's complete cluelessness while being oddly helpful.

jokes.text: Yeah, I mean, somebody like you, or you.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn talking about needing someone 'like' David to help organize
jokes.punchline: The transparent manipulation reveal when she adds 'or you'
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection structure - starts as a compliment, reveals as manipulation. Great economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: What just happened?
jokes.setup: David has just been manipulated into organizing Jocelyn's baby shower tonight
jokes.punchline: His delayed realization of being completely outmaneuvered
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing David's confusion at being manipulated, though fairly standard structure.

jokes.text: Okay um, but wouldn't it make more sense to offer a discount for people not dining alone?
jokes.setup: Alexis suggests 50% discount for singles dining alone during Singles Week
jokes.punchline: Twyla pointing out the logical flaw in the promotion
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor showing Twyla's practical intelligence versus Alexis's scattered thinking.

jokes.text: A 2-for-1 discount for 2 singles eating together. That's it, that's the winner. Yay!
jokes.setup: Discussion about Singles Week promotion ideas
jokes.punchline: Alexis triumphantly announcing an idea that's exactly what Twyla just suggested
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - she's completely oblivious that she's restating Twyla's idea while acting like she invented it.

jokes.text: Oh my god, what are you doing here? - Oh. - Okay, so... me, Albany, Jitney, and Candy, we just needed to get away from the city for a bit, and so Vice put out this guide to the most random cities in North America, and we were like, lets him 'em.
jokes.setup: Alexis running into her friend from her old life
jokes.punchline: Learning they came here because it was listed as one of the most random cities
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the show's premise - the town is so backwater that it's literally a tourist destination for randomness. Great character integration showing Alexis's past life.

jokes.text: This is where you live, isn't it? I'm so sorry I didn't put that together, it's like I completely forgot about you.
jokes.setup: Klaire realizing this is where Alexis lives
jokes.punchline: The brutal honesty that she 'completely forgot about' Alexis
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny moment showing how completely Alexis has fallen off her old social circle's radar. The casual cruelty is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Oh my god, I'm obsessed with your look. You look just like a small-town waitress! Beep!
jokes.setup: Klaire commenting on Alexis's appearance
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastically complimenting her for looking exactly like what she actually is
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - Klaire thinks Alexis is cosplaying poverty chic, not realizing this is actually her life now. The 'Beep!' adds perfect shallow-friend energy.

jokes.text: Actually you know what I would love like a hot water, but if you could just let it sit so it cools, I just need to know that it was once hot. Sorry, I'm the worst.
jokes.setup: Klaire ordering something to drink
jokes.punchline: The completely ridiculous request for water that was hot but is now cool
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist humor - captures the pretentious, performative nature of wellness culture. The specificity makes it hilarious, and 'I just need to know that it was once hot' is perfectly quotable.

jokes.text: Albany literally makes me wanna kill myself.
jokes.setup: Klaire talking about needing distraction
jokes.punchline: Casual suicidal ideation over mild annoyance
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Klaire's dramatic personality and casual cruelty toward supposed friends.

jokes.text: Sweetie, no, I didn't order that.
jokes.setup: Alexis brings Klaire the hot-water-that-cooled order
jokes.punchline: Klaire denying she ordered it after making such a specific request
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Klaire's casual cruelty and entitlement. Shows how she makes elaborate demands then dismisses the server entirely.

jokes.text: See you tonight, babe. See you tonight, babe. Hm. Hm.
jokes.setup: Klaire has left after casual dismissal
jokes.punchline: Alexis processing the toxic interaction by repeating the phrase
jokes.explanation: Shows Alexis's emotional intelligence - she knows something's off but can't quite articulate it. The repetition captures confusion.

jokes.text: Mature Adults Dating Lovers, Yes!
jokes.setup: Johnny asks what MADLY stands for
jokes.punchline: The awkward acronym that doesn't quite work and sounds vaguely sexual
jokes.explanation: Funny forced acronym that captures the awkwardness of dating services trying to sound both professional and sexy.

jokes.text: Well, we could peek in on the experience like group voyeurs, but it would be much more educational for both of you if you would let me immerse you in it.
jokes.setup: Moira and Johnny want to observe the dating event
jokes.punchline: Ricki's inappropriate use of 'group voyeurs' and pushy sales tactics
jokes.explanation: Ricki's inappropriate word choice and pushy facilitation style creates good character comedy.

jokes.text: Well, lucky for us, this lock has already found its key. - You'd be surprised. - Would I?
jokes.setup: Johnny confidently states he and Moira are already matched
jokes.punchline: Ricki's ominous suggestion that even married couples might not be compatible
jokes.explanation: Great exchange showing the facilitator's cynical worldview clashing with Johnny's confidence. 'Would I?' is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: I just have to ask, before the event, will you grant me access to your most intimate sounds? - No. But we'll play the game.
jokes.setup: Ricki asking for permission for something vaguely sexual-sounding
jokes.punchline: Moira's immediate, firm 'No' followed by practical compromise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - her absolute boundary-setting followed by practical willingness to participate. Great economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: Well, um, Jocelyn came in here this morning looking like Adam Sandler on a red carpet.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he volunteered to help Jocelyn
jokes.punchline: The specific, devastating comparison to Adam Sandler on a red carpet
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant comparison - captures both the inappropriateness and the specific type of fashion disaster. Perfect economy and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Well, typically the person who throws the shower, pays for the shower. - Well, that wasn't part of the agreement. - Well, what did you say to her? - I told her I'd take care of it. - Ah!
jokes.setup: David realizing he's been financially manipulated
jokes.punchline: His weak defense followed by Stevie's knowing 'Ah!'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing David's naivety and Stevie's knowing reaction to his predictable mistake.

jokes.text: Oh, so it's more of a sprinkle, then. - What the hell is a sprinkle? - No, it's like a shower, but for your second kid. It's not a full shower, it's like a sprinkle.
jokes.setup: Learning this isn't Jocelyn's first baby
jokes.punchline: David's outrage at the concept of a 'sprinkle' and Stevie's matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about parenting culture terminology, with David's characteristic outrage at social customs.

jokes.text: That is the stupidest (bleep) thing I have ever heard.
jokes.setup: Learning about the concept of baby sprinkles
jokes.punchline: David's over-the-top outrage at a harmless social custom
jokes.explanation: Classic David overreaction to minor social customs. The censored swear adds emphasis to his outrage.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, she's one person, and everyone knows you don't plan a whole party around one person. - Maybe you do, if the party is for her. - Says who?
jokes.setup: David complaining about accommodating the pregnant woman at her own baby shower
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to grasp the basic concept of whose party it is
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - his self-centered worldview is so complete he can't understand why a baby shower should accommodate the pregnant woman. 'Says who?' is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: This is why I hate babies!
jokes.setup: David's mounting frustration with party planning constraints
jokes.punchline: His dramatic conclusion that blames innocent babies for his problems
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character comedy - David's tendency to blame external forces for problems he created. Good quotability.

jokes.text: So it would be you, and my dad's ex-wife. But don't worry about her, she's two years younger than us, and like super chill.
jokes.setup: Describing living situation
jokes.punchline: Stepmom younger than the daughter presenting this as normal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures toxic wealthy family dynamics - the age detail makes the dysfunction concrete and funny.

jokes.text: Hey, pack your bags, bitch! You're getting' out!
jokes.setup: Klaire has been talking about firing Albany all episode
jokes.punchline: Her casual, aggressive delivery of the firing
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the running gag about Albany, showing Klaire's casual cruelty in a different context.

jokes.text: I know you're gonna find this hard to believe, but she predicted that I would meet a woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, and red lipstick. - Telepathic, was she? - And tremendously specific!
jokes.setup: Man explains his dead wife's prediction about his next relationship
jokes.punchline: The hilariously generic description that could fit thousands of women
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about the man's complete lack of self-awareness about how generic his 'specific' description is.

jokes.text: Maybe I'll spend the night sometime. At the motel, I mean. - Oh, oh, oh.
jokes.setup: Charlene making an awkward advance on Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira's uncomfortable realization of the implication
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward flirtation moment with good character reaction from Moira.

jokes.text: I thought I would what I could to get a jumpstart on the decorations.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explaining her decoration efforts
jokes.punchline: The implication that her 'jumpstart' decorations are horrifyingly bad
jokes.explanation: Sets up the visual gag of terrible decorations and Jocelyn's well-meaning incompetence.

jokes.text: Maybe to the garbage?
jokes.setup: Jocelyn asking if she should move some balloons
jokes.punchline: David's polite but devastating suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect David diplomacy - finding the politest way to say something is terrible. Great economy.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, is that supposed to be a baby? - Yeah, it's pretty realistic, isn't it? I got it at the dollar store in Elmdale. I didn't even have to pay for it, honey. They just gave it to me.
jokes.setup: David seeing Roland's baby piñata
jokes.punchline: Roland's pride in the free, dollar-store baby piñata and its 'realism'
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy setup with Roland's complete lack of awareness about appropriate baby shower activities. The fact that it was free makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Yeah, don't think we can smash a baby with a stick tonight, but...
jokes.setup: Roland suggesting they use the baby piñata
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact observation about why this is inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - stating the obvious problem in the most direct way possible. Great economy and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Gonna stop you right there. I will literally do anything for us to not play that game.
jokes.setup: Hearing about the diaper chocolate game
jokes.punchline: David's immediate, desperate intervention
jokes.explanation: Perfect David reaction to gross-out humor. His immediate boundary-setting is very in character.

jokes.text: Oh my gosh, it is so fun! We played it at Mutt's baby shower! Basically, you melt different chocolate bars into different diapers, and then people take turns guessing...
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explaining the diaper game enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: The horrifying realization of what this 'fun' game involves
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about terrible baby shower games. Jocelyn's enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I am ready.
jokes.setup: David suggesting Jocelyn should get changed for the party
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact response while presumably looking terrible
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the 'Adam Sandler on a red carpet' description. Jocelyn's obliviousness to her appearance is perfect.

jokes.text: Uh, no, that there is grandpa's ashes, and no, you cannot move it, it's bolted down. There's been a lot of urn thefts lately.
jokes.setup: David asking if a vase can be moved
jokes.punchline: Learning it's grandpa's ashes, bolted down due to urn thefts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdism - each revelation is more bizarre than the last. 'Urn thefts' is such a specific, weird crime.

jokes.text: Where do I put this (bleep) thing?
jokes.setup: David is handed the baby piñata
jokes.punchline: His frustrated confusion about what to do with the inappropriate decoration
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the piñata problem, showing David's mounting frustration with the whole situation.

jokes.text: I work in ad sales and can drive at night.
jokes.setup: Rene introducing himself to Moira
jokes.punchline: His bizarre combination of professional and driving credentials as pickup lines
jokes.explanation: Absolutely bizarre pickup line that shows this character's complete social ineptitude. The specificity makes it hilarious.

jokes.text: I know that sounds like an odd thing to mention, but it does separate me from the crowd.
jokes.setup: After mentioning he can drive at night
jokes.punchline: His earnest belief that night driving is a rare and attractive skill
jokes.explanation: Great character work showing his complete misunderstanding of what makes someone attractive. His earnestness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Yes, we have, but here, locks sadly are defective.
jokes.setup: Rene asking if they've found their match after struggling with the lock
jokes.punchline: Moira diplomatically blaming the equipment rather than rejecting him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - her elaborate way of rejecting someone while maintaining politeness. Very character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Often times in these situations, I ask a participant to go within, and to reflect why she might be having trouble connecting.
jokes.setup: Moira having trouble with the lock game
jokes.punchline: Therapist suggesting deep self-reflection over party game failure
jokes.explanation: Typical wellness culture - turning mechanical failure into deep psychological analysis. Classic oversharing therapist.

jokes.text: Now mommy's had a very hard day with baby, and needs a bit of a break. And that's where we come in. We're each gonna take turns popping pills into mommy's mouth.
jokes.setup: David explaining a new baby shower game
jokes.punchline: The disturbing implications of the game he's invented
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor disguised as innocent fun. The casual way David describes drugging a new mother is perfectly twisted.

jokes.text: Now unfortunately, these are breath mints, we couldn't afford actual benzo's
jokes.setup: Continuing to explain the 'sleepy mommy' game
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning they would have used real drugs if they could afford them
jokes.explanation: Peak dark comedy - the casual admission that budget constraints prevented them from actually drugging the pregnant woman. Absolutely brilliant and quotable.

jokes.text: You invented it. We played it a lot growing up. - Oh! I can't remember playing. - That's because we got very good at it.
jokes.setup: Johnny recognizing the pill-popping game
jokes.punchline: The dark implication about their family dynamics and David's matter-of-fact delivery
jokes.explanation: Absolutely masterful dark comedy revealing the Rose family's dysfunctional past. The escalating reveals and David's deadpan delivery create perfect horror/comedy. This is writing at its finest.

jokes.text: I remember Alexis got one in from across the room once.
jokes.setup: Continuing the revelation about their childhood pill game
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of Alexis's skill at drugging their father
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation to the previous joke, adding more disturbing detail while maintaining the casual tone. Shows the depth of the family's dysfunction.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, reset. What are we talking about?
jokes.setup: Alexis declining the job offer Klaire made earlier
jokes.punchline: Klaire's complete inability to remember offering the job
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Klaire's casual cruelty and short attention span. The job that seemed so important is now completely forgotten.

jokes.text: Breaking news, we still (bleep) hate her.
jokes.setup: Talking about Albany throughout the episode
jokes.punchline: The mock news announcement about their ongoing hatred
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the running gag about Albany. The 'breaking news' format adds energy to the casual cruelty.
